sanskrit:
इति गुह्यतमं शास्त्रमिदमुक्तं मयानघ।
एतद्बुद्ध्वा बुद्धिमान्स्यात्कृतकृत्यश्च भारत।।
transliteration:
iti guhyatamaṃ śāstram idam uktaṃ mayānagha |
etad buddhvā buddhimān syāt kṛta-kṛtyaś ca bhārata ||
literal_meaning: Thus the most secret teaching has been spoken by Me, O sinless one; knowing this, one becomes wise and has fulfilled all duties, O descendant of Bharata.
contextual_meaning: Krishna closes the chapter by branding the entire vision of Puruṣottama-yoga as the apex of all psychological insight: when the mind finally internalizes that its core identity is the eternal witness (kṣetrajña) linked to the Supreme Person, the search for self-worth outside ceases; the personality reorganizes around inner security, rendering every other life-task auxiliary.
psychological_state: Post-insight consolidation: the calm that follows a major cognitive re-structuring; ego no longer needs to prove itself, yet vigilance against regression remains.
trigger_scenario: A client realizes in therapy that their lifelong anxiety was driven by a single core belief (“I am not enough”); when that belief collapses, they feel both elation and the vertigo of having to live without the old narrative.
anchor_text: Know yourself as the eternal witness and every duty quietly falls into place.
